User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the SprintSync Board application, including their role and contact information. Passwords and authentication details are managed by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: The user's chosen username for logging in and display.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and potentially login.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, determining their permissions (e.g., 'admin' for teachers, 'member' for students).

rating.description: A 1-5 star rating assigned to the user (typically students) by an administrator.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Board.title: Board
Board.description: Represents a Kanban board where tasks are organized and tracked.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Board entity.

title.description: The title of the Kanban board.

description.description: An optional detailed description of the board's purpose or project.

ownerId.description: Reference to the User who created and owns this board. (Relationship: User 1:N Board)

memberIds.description: References to Users who are members of this board and can access/contribute to it. (Relationship: User N:N Board)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the board was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the board was last updated.

Column.title: Column
Column.description: Represents a column (or status stage) within a Kanban board, such as 'New', 'In Progress', or 'Done'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Column entity.

boardId.description: Reference to the Board this column belongs to. (Relationship: Board 1:N Column)

title.description: The title of the column (e.g., 'To Do', 'In Development', 'Resolved').

order.description: The display order of the column within its board, allowing for custom arrangement.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the column was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the column was last updated.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a single task or work item on a Kanban board, including its details, assignee, and current status (determined by its column).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

columnId.description: Reference to the Column this task is currently placed in, indicating its current status. (Relationship: Column 1:N Task)

boardId.description: Reference to the Board this task belongs to, for direct board-level filtering. (Relationship: Board 1:N Task, derived from Column)

title.description: A concise title for the task.

description.description: A detailed description of the task, potentially generated or enhanced by AI.

assigneeId.description: Optional reference to the User assigned to this task. (Relationship: User 1:N Task)

dueDate.description: Optional date and time when the task is due.

subtasks.description: An array of sub-tasks or action items associated with the main task, potentially AI-suggested.

acceptanceCriteria.description: An array of criteria that must be met for the task to be considered complete, potentially AI-generated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the task was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the task was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, including their global role ('admin' for teachers, 'member' for students). Path-based ownership, accessible by the user themselves or by admins for listing.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Represents a Kanban board. Includes 'ownerId' and 'memberIds' map for direct authorization checks, supporting collaborative access. These fields are denormalized into subcollections for authorization independence.

params.name: boardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Kanban board.

definition.description: Represents a column within a board. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'memberIds' (copied from the parent Board) for authorization independence.

params.name: boardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent board.

params.name: columnId
params.description: The unique identifier of the column.

definition.description: Represents a task within a column. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'memberIds' (copied from the grandparent Board) for authorization independence.

params.name: boardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent board.

params.name: columnId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent column.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier of the task.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Accurate Permissions (QAPs) by strategically denormalizing authorization context into subcollection documents. User roles are managed directly within the `/users/{userId}` documents, making global role checks simple and efficient using `get(/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role`. This adheres to the DBAC principle.

For collaborative entities like 'Board', the `ownerId` and `memberIds` are stored directly on the `/boards/{boardId}` document. This allows for straightforward security rules where `request.auth.uid` must be either the `ownerId` or included in the `memberIds` array. To extend this authorization to nested entities (`Column` and `Task`) without resorting to expensive `get()` calls on parent documents, the `ownerId` and `memberIds` from the `Board` are **denormalized** into every `Column` and `Task` document. This is critical for maintaining authorization independence across the hierarchy, simplifying rules, and enabling atomic operations (e.g., creating a board with its initial columns in a single batch).

**Authorization Independence:**
- Global roles ('admin'/'member') are checked by reading the `role` field on the current user's `/users/{request.auth.uid}` document. This is a direct, self-contained check.
- For `Columns` and `Tasks`, their documents contain copies of `ownerId` and `memberIds` from their respective parent `Board`. This means a security rule on `/boards/{boardId}/columns/{columnId}` can directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId || request.auth.uid in resource.data.memberIds` without needing to fetch the parent `Board` document. The same applies to `Tasks` which denormalize from their grandparent `Board`.

**Query-Accurate Permissions (QAPs):**
- For `/users/{userId}`: An 'admin' user (teacher) can perform `list` operations, allowing them to view all user profiles for their 'all members board'. Regular 'member' users (students) can only `read` their own profile. The rules will enforce this. Applications must use `where()` clauses for specific user queries (e.g., `where('role', '==', 'student')`).
- For `/boards/{boardId}`: Users can only `list` boards where they are either the `ownerId` or one of the `memberIds`. The application must always filter its queries (e.g., `boards.where('ownerId', '==', request.auth.uid).get()` or `boards.where('memberIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid).get()`). The security rules ensure that *every* document returned by such a query satisfies the `ownerId` or `memberIds` condition, preventing data leakage if a malicious query attempts to bypass client-side filtering.
- For `/boards/{boardId}/columns/{columnId}` and `/boards/{boardId}/columns/{columnId}/tasks/{taskId}`: Similar to boards, `list` operations on these subcollections are implicitly guarded by the denormalized `ownerId` and `memberIds`. Any `list` query must match these fields, and the security rules will verify each returned document against `request.auth.uid` for these denormalized fields, ensuring only authorized columns/tasks are returned.
